summary.intent: Push a bug-fix commit (50ee1026) to PR #710 on fogodev/ars-ui, reply to and resolve 8 Codex review threads, trigger a new Codex review pass, and get CI fully green.
summary.outcome: All 8 Codex threads were replied to and resolved, a new @codex review was triggered, and a follow-up commit was pushed to fix two Clippy lints (const helper and struct-update-syntax in tests) that broke CI on Clippy 1.96.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask ci clippy` as the canonical Clippy gate that mirrors CI — running this locally before pushing catches lint regressions before they hit CI.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment; re-triggering after resolving threads is part of the normal review loop.
The Feature Flag Check CI job is gated on Clippy — it gets skipped/fails whenever Clippy fails, making it appear as a secondary failure.

code.finding: Clippy 1.96 flagged a helper function that could be `const` — making it const satisfies the lint.
Clippy 1.96 flagged a test `Env` initialization that should use struct update syntax instead of field-by-field initialization.

workflow: The GitHub shell loop `for pair in 'A B'` trick doesn't reliably split pairs — use separate variables or indexed arrays when iterating over compound values in bash.
The correct endpoint for replying to a PR review comment is `POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/comments/{comment_id}/replies`, not the generic comments endpoint.
GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation requires the thread node ID (e.g. `PRRT_kwDORp4enM6GjaZD`), not the comment database ID.

friction: Initial GitHub API loop failed silently due to shell argument parsing — each `'A B'` pair was treated as a single argument, so `$1`/`$2` split incorrectly.
First retry still failed because the loop structure was wrong and an outdated reply endpoint shape was used.
GitHub connection errors during the first reply/resolve loop required a full retry cycle.
CI failure (Clippy + Feature Flag Check) was caused by Clippy 1.96 enforcing two new lints not caught by local test runs, requiring an unplanned follow-up commit.
open_items: Second Codex review pass was triggered but results were not yet available at the end of the session — needs follow-up to confirm no new threads were opened.
CI status after the Clippy fix commit was not confirmed green — the push was made but job outcomes were not verified.
